# ANALYSIS OF OUTPUTS GENERATED BY DIFFERENT TYPES OF PROMPTING TECHNIQUES

# Zero-shot
- Requirement: Useful when simple and short queries are being asked
- Strength: strict schema improved structure
- Weakness: model sometimes adds extra explanation

# Role-shot
- Requirement: Useful when a customized or specific output is needed
- Strength: relevant information and better context 
- Weakness: sometimes model can miss some of the aspects because of assigned role boundaries

# One-shot
- Requirement: Useful when output of the query needs to be formatted
- Strength: improved numerical reasoning
- Weakness: occasional overconfidence in assumptions

# Few-shot
- Requirement: Useful when output needs to be fetched in a highly specific format
- Strength: better boundary learning
- Weakness: still struggles with ambiguous tickets

# Chain-of-thought
- Requirement: Useful when step by step formatted output is needed
- Strength: improved procedure solving and complex tasks become easy to handle
- Weakness: can generate very lengthy output and sometimes irrelevant steps

# Tree-of-thought
- Requirement: When multiple answers are needed for analysis
- Strength: improved suggestions and best, most relevant outcome can be retrieved
- Weakness: sometimes can generate unnecessary and irrelevant suggestions/outputs and finding out  the best answer can be confusing and time taking.
